data.body:
## Context
The DSO plugin's largest bug category is workflow/multi-script interactions (34 fix commits in the last month) — bugs where the issue is how scripts interact through the worktree lifecycle: ticket init → sprint → commit with hooks → merge-to-main → version bump. These bugs are deterministic but only manifest when the full workflow chain executes in sequence. A Docker-based E2E test suite that simulates complete development sessions in a CI-equivalent environment would catch these integration failures as test regressions.

## Success Criteria
- A Dockerfile produces an Ubuntu-based test environment matching CI (same git/Python versions, no global git config)
- E2E tests exercise the complete worktree lifecycle: worktree creation → ticket init → ticket create/transition/compact/comment → commit with hooks → merge-to-main → version bump → verify main state (git log, ticket state, version file)
- E2E tests exercise cross-cutting scenarios: compact-on-close followed by comment/edit/transition on compacted ticket
- Tests run in CI only via a dedicated GitHub Actions workflow
- LLM-dependent hooks bypassed via DSO_E2E_MODE=1: review gate and test gate exit 0 unconditionally; PreToolUse sentinels absent (no Claude Code runtime in Docker); non-LLM hook logic (formatting, linting, git) executes normally
- Structured diagnostic output on failure: step name, expected vs actual, git log at failure point, ticket state dump
- At least one multi-worktree test: two worktrees with separate tickets merging sequentially, verifying main contains both changes
- At least 3 of the 34 recent workflow bug fixes are reproducible as E2E test cases

## Dependencies
None

## Approach
Full workflow simulation in Docker. Tests exercise the complete worktree development lifecycle with deterministic LLM bypass (DSO_E2E_MODE=1). Runs in CI only — too expensive for local test gate. Focuses on the workflow/multi-script interaction category that accounts for 34 of the last month's fix commits.
